role: Member of Technical Staff (Model Behavior Architect)
hero_headline: An answer engine is only as good as how its agents behave. I architect that behavior — and I built this page 19 minutes after seeing the role.
hero_subline: I run a fleet of autonomous agents in production: 7 specialized agents over a file-based bus, reading email every 2h and shipping real output via API. Model behavior, evals, and guardrails are the job I already do daily — for Perplexity I'd do it at frontier scale.
pitch_oneliner: Week 1: instrument behavior evals on a live agent surface (Comet-style browse → act) and ship the first guardrail that moves a real metric — not a deck about one.

jd_match.requirement: Shape and evaluate model behavior across agentic, multi-step tasks
jd_match.proof: Production multi-agent system (empresaAI) — 7 agents coordinating over a file-based bus, with deterministic state machines and behavior guardrails so they act, not just chat.
2 years shipping RAG + LLM features (OpenAI 3y, Anthropic 2y); retrieval-grounded agents that cite sources and degrade gracefully when retrieval fails.

jd_match.requirement: Builder who ships in weeks, not quarters
jd_match.proof: ManoloBot (manoloelrobot.com) — an autonomous robot that builds and deploys one production app per day, end to end, publicly.
MSc Industrial Engineering (UPM, Operations Research). I reason about agent behavior as a control problem: inputs, feedback loops, failure modes.
